homepage.apps.description.message: Because Owncast is built on open standards you can watch an Owncast-powered stream on any device. But if you want, we've built some easy ways to watch that work out of the box.

homepage.apps.ios.description.message: Browse the directory, add private servers, and get notified when streams go live on the iPhone.

homepage.apps.roku.description.message: The Roku channel lets you browse the Owncasst directory and add private servers. A very convenient way to watch live streams on the most popular set-top box.

homepage.apps.appletv.description.message: For users of AppleTV, this app provides an easy way to access the Owncast directory and your favorite streams right from your TV.

homepage.archetypes.independents.descrip.message: Creators who want full control over their streaming presence.

homepage.archetypes.groups.description.message: Organizations that want a space they own to connect and broadcast together.

homepage.archetypes.builders.description.message: Developers who want to self-host and customize their streaming.

homepage.archetypes.enterprises.descript.message: Businesses powering events, apps, and broadcasts with live video.

homepage.archetypes.subtitle.message: Owncast works for all kinds of streams. Here's some of who's using it.

homepage.faq.description.message: Answers to common questions people ask about Owncast.

homepage.faq.q1.question.message: Should I move to Owncast from Twitch, YouTube, Facebook, or another platform?

homepage.faq.q1.answer.message:
Honestly, {probablyNot} Owncast is an alternative for when those platforms no longer meet your needs. If you are happy with your current platform, and the tradeoffs involved, there's no need to switch.

But if you're building a project that requires a live streaming component, or have an internal streaming requirement, or you're a media company who wants to create a 24/7 stream, then Owncast makes sense where other options do not.

Or if you're somebody who has found other platforms no longer welcoming to you and your content, or you want to go a different direction, then building your own thing, taking your existing audience with you, and being responsible for your own destiny also makes a lot of sense.

homepage.faq.q2.question.message: Do the Owncast developers know what I'm streaming?

homepage.faq.q2.answer.message: By default, {absolutelyNot} There is no way for anybody to know this but you and your viewers. However, if you choose to list your live stream on the {directoryLink}, or have the {socialLink} on the Fediverse, then more information about your content will be available to others.

homepage.faq.q3.question.message: Is it expensive to run Owncast? Somebody on Twitch said it's really expensive.

homepage.faq.q3.answer.message:
Owncast can run at a low-cost and run on modest hardware. Even a modern Raspberry Pi. It's generally {resourcesLink} to pay. {costEmphasis}

Most people begin with a small server or even self-host on their own hardware. Starting with a simple configuration keeps things manageable. As your audience grows, you can {scalingLink} your hosting solution accordingly to manage increased traffic. Moving to {storageLink} generally {removes} most bandwidth concerns.

homepage.faq.q4.question.message: Is it hard to set up Owncast? Somebody on YouTube said it's really hard.

homepage.faq.q4.answer.message: Owncast is designed to be user-friendly and straightforward to set up. Our documentation provides {quickstartLink} to help you get started quickly.

homepage.faq.q5.question.message: I want to grow my audience. Can Owncast get me more viewers? TikTok gives me more viewers.

homepage.faq.q5.answer.fediverse.message: Connect to the {fediverseLink}, allowing your stream to be discovered by users on Mastodon, Pleroma, and Misskey. Encourage people to follow and share your stream.

homepage.faq.q5.answer.directory.message: List your stream in the {directoryLink}, making it discoverable by people browsing for new streams.

homepage.faq.q5.answer.conclusion.message: Owncast may not be a good fit if you're looking to press "Go Live" and expect people to just show up.

homepage.faq.q6.question.message: I'm a representative of a media company looking to take down a live stream that says it's run by Owncast. Take it down, or else!

homepage.faq.q6.answer.message: {youProblem} Owncast is not involved and cannot be of any help to you. You'd be surprised how many people refuse to understand this.

homepage.faq.q7.question.message: Where can I chat with people about Owncast if I have questions?

homepage.faq.q7.answer.message: If our {docsLink} isn't answering your question, visit our {contactLink} for links to community chat rooms, issue trackers, and other ways to get in touch with the Owncast community and developers.

homepage.features.customAppearance.descr.message: Quickly pick colors, or go completely custom by writing your own CSS to style your Owncast page to look any way you like. It runs on your own domain, so it has your identity.

homepage.features.softwareOwn.title.message: Software you own, not a service you rent

homepage.features.softwareOwn.descriptio.message: Download it without any registration or sign-up, and it's yours to run anywhere you like. It can be your existing corporate infrastructure, a cloud provider, or even your own computer at home. Once you download it, we're no longer involved.

homepage.features.worksEverywhere.descri.message: Not every live stream's goal is to get maximum exposure. Owncast lets you stream privately or to a limited audience, perfect for corporate events, private gatherings, or sensitive content.

homepage.features.multiDevice.title.message: Watch on different devices and players

homepage.features.multiDevice.descriptio.message: Because Owncast is built on open standards, you can watch your stream on a variety of devices and media players. Including custom applications you build yourself for mobile, the web, smart TVs, and more.

homepage.features.privacy.description.message: No tracking, no accounts required, no data harvesting. You and your viewers can stay anonymous while still being part of the community. When you run your own services, you don't have to identify yourself.

homepage.features.fediverse.description.message: Owncast integrates with the Fediverse, allowing you to connect and interact with a broader community across decentralized social networks.

homepage.features.sectionDescription.message: There are many different reasons for using Owncast. Here are some common ones.

homepage.featurePreview.chat.description.message: Real-time chat included, no third-party services required. Moderation tools, custom emotes, and user authentication included.

homepage.featurePreview.branding.descrip.message: Stream from your own domain with your own look and feel. Create your own UI, truly owning the experience.

homepage.featurePreview.notifications.de.message: Notify followers via various channels when you go live. Never miss an opportunity to connect with your audience.

homepage.featurePreview.fediverse.descri.message: Encourage engagement and reach new viewers across the fediverse. Connect with Mastodon and ActivityPub platforms.

homepage.featurePreview.extensible.descr.message: Integrates with existing tools, and build your own internal and external utilities on top of the API.

homepage.hero.title.message: Open source software for running your own live stream

homepage.hero.description.message: It runs on your server and works with standard streaming protocols. There are no subscriptions, viewer limits, or forced ads. Flexible, customizable, and it's as private or public as you want it to be.

homepage.installer.keypoint1.description.message: We won't expect you to clone a git repo, install dependencies, and understand the development environment it was built with by making you fight with npm, pip or ruby gems.

homepage.installer.keypoint2.title.message: Choose how you want to install it

homepage.installer.keypoint2.description.message: Use our installer, download the single file yourself, or spin up a Docker container. Otherwise, sign up for a hosting provider that will install it for you automatically.

homepage.installer.keypoint3.title.message: Start simple. Increase complexity as you need it

homepage.installer.keypoint3.description.message: If you're jumping in for the first time, start with a simple setup. As you get more comfortable, you can explore advanced configurations and optimizations.

homepage.installer.title.message: Install in seconds. Stream in minutes.

homepage.protocolCompat.description.message: Choose the tools, services, and software of your choice to work with Owncast.

homepage.softwareCompat.title.message: Compatible with your favorite broadcasting tools

homepage.softwareCompat.description.message: Point your broadcast at your new Owncast server and you'll be live using a server you control.

homepage.sponsors.description.message: These organizations support Owncast via non-monetary support and services.

homepage.store.description.message: Support the project and show your Owncast pride. Each purchase supports the open source project.

contributors.description.message: Contribute in technical, or non-technical ways.

donors.description.message: Help support the project by making a financial donation at

donors.note.message: This list reflects active financial supporters helping fund ongoing development, initiatives and infrastructure.

docs.welcome.description.message: Owncast is a self-hosted live video and web chat server that allows you to stream your content to your audience without relying on third-party platforms. Whether you're a beginner looking to set up your first stream or an experienced user seeking advanced configuration options, this documentation aims to point you in the right direction.

chat.community.chooseName.message: Choose a name to join the chat

chat.community.connecting.message: Connecting to the Owncast support chat...

chat.community.description.message: This is a community chat room where you can ask questions and get help from other Owncast users and contributors.

chat.community.guidelines.message: Please be courteous and respectful of others who may be volunteering their time to help. Before asking, consider checking the {docsLink} and {issuesLink} to see if your question has already been answered.

chat.info.subtitle.message: A place to ask questions and get help from the Owncast community.

chat.info.body.message: This is a community chat room where you can connect with other Owncast users and contributors. Please be courteous and respectful of others who volunteer their time to help.

chat.info.checkFirst.message: Before asking, consider checking the {docsLink} and {issuesLink} to see if your question has already been answered.

chat.info.matrix.description.message: This chat is powered by {matrixLink}, an open, decentralized communication protocol. You can join this room using any Matrix client:

chat.answers.subtitle.message: Answers from the Owncast documentation and past issues

chat.answers.welcome.message: Hi, I'm Owncat, I can search existing Owncast {docsLink} to try to find answers for you. As automated results, these are not replacements for reading the docs yourself or talking to real people, but could be a useful tool.

chat.answers.askCommunity.message: Need more help? Ask the community →

chat.displayName.errorFailed.message: Failed to set name. Please try again.

chat.displayName.descriptionChange.message: Update the name shown with your messages.

directory.editPage.message: Have resources to share? Improve this page by adding them.

chat.answers.welcomeAlternatives.message: If you'd prefer to {chatLink}, you can do that too, though you may have to wait for someone to respond. You're also welcome to file questions on our {issueTracker}.

chat.answers.welcomeChatLink.message: chat with real Owncast developers and community members

chat.community.welcome.message: You're now in the Owncast support chat. Say hello!

resourcesCalculator.rec.highCpu.message: High CPU usage detected. Consider reducing quality variants or lowering frame rates.

resourcesCalculator.rec.buffering.message: {experienceText} may experience buffering. Add a lower bitrate quality option for mobile and slower network viewers.

resourcesCalculator.rec.s3.message: Consider using object storage to scale bandwidth with more viewers and reduce server load.

resourcesCalculator.rec.highBitrateNoLow.message: High bitrate without low quality options will exclude viewers on mobile and slower networks. Add a quality variant below 2000 kbps.

resourcesCalculator.rec.singleHighQualit.message: Single quality streams above 4000 kbps may cause buffering for viewers on mobile and slower networks. Add lower quality variants.

resourcesCalculator.rec.passthroughOnly.message: Passthrough mode saves CPU but may cause buffering for viewers on slower connections.

resourcesCalculator.rec.highFrameRate.message: High frame rates (60+ fps) significantly increase CPU usage. Consider lowering for non-gaming content.

resourcesCalculator.rec.veryHighFrameRat.message: 90 fps requires substantial CPU resources. Only recommended for high-motion content with powerful hardware.

resourcesCalculator.rec.lowerFrameRate.message: Consider adding a lower frame rate option (24 or 15 fps) to reduce CPU usage and improve experience for mobile and slower network viewers.

resourcesCalculator.rec.mixedFrameRates.message: Lower quality variants could use reduced frame rates (24-30 fps) to save CPU while maintaining good experience.

resourcesCalculator.editor.passthroughFr.message: Frame rate will match input stream

wizard.quickstart.pages.install-method-p.message: How do you want to install Owncast?

By using the installer you can get up and running in about a minute. However, if you would prefer to download a release manually, use a one-click install at a supported hosting provider, use a container you have those options and more. If you'd prefer you can browse our entire installation documentation.
I want to manually install Owncast myself.
I want Owncast to be installed for me on a hosting provider.
I want to run Owncast inside a containerized environment such as Docker or Kubernetes.
Other specific hardware or hosting environments.
Owncast is installed and I'm not sure what to do next.

wizard.quickstart.pages.docker-result.el.message:
Docker Installation Guide

Follow the Docker installation guide

This guide will walk you through running Owncast in a containerized environment.

wizard.quickstart.pages.manual-install-m.message: How would you like to manually install Owncast?
Owncast supplies a quick install script that will always install the most recent version and get you running very quickly. However, there are other options.
I want to use the Owncast Quick Installer Script.
I want to download a release of Owncast.
I want to use my operating system's package manager.

wizard.quickstart.pages.quickinstaller-r.message:
Quick Installer Guide

Use the Owncast Quick Installer

The quick installer will download and set up Owncast for you automatically.

wizard.quickstart.pages.download-result..message:
Manual Download Guide

Download and install Owncast manually

This guide will show you how to download and set up Owncast step by step.

wizard.quickstart.pages.os-package-manag.message: Use your Operating System's package manager

Note: These packages may be out of date. These packages are not built or maintained by the Owncast project and any specific concerns or support regarding their installation should be directed at the maintainers of the packages.

What Operating System or distribution do you use?

wizard.quickstart.pages.installed-what-i.message: If Owncast is installed and running but you're not sure what to do next you might be ready to go! But there are other things you might want to check such as making sure you support SSL.
What would you like to do next?
I want to learn how to start streaming.
My server doesn't yet support SSL (https) and I want to configure that.
I want to setup Owncast to automatically start and run in the background as a service.
I want to list my stream in the public directory so others can discover it.
I want to learn more about configuring my video quality.

wizard.quickstart.pages.startstreaming-r.message:
Broadcasting Guide

Learn how to start streaming

This guide covers how to configure your broadcasting software and start streaming to your Owncast server.

wizard.quickstart.pages.configure-ssl-re.message:
SSL Configuration Guide

Configure SSL and reverse proxies

Learn how to set up SSL/TLS encryption for your Owncast server.

wizard.quickstart.pages.configure-system.message:
System Service Guide

Configure Owncast as a system service

Set up Owncast to automatically start and run in the background.

wizard.quickstart.pages.directory-result.message:
Directory Listing Guide

List your stream in the directory

Learn how to make your stream discoverable in the public directory.

wizard.quickstart.pages.configure-video-.message:
Video Configuration Guide

Configure video quality and settings

Learn how to optimize your video quality and configure encoding settings.

wizard.troubleshooting.pages.firstpage.t.message: What issue are you trying to resolve?

wizard.troubleshooting.pages.firstpage.d.message: While each environment is different, hopefully many questions and issues can be answered by walking through the following topics.

wizard.troubleshooting.pages.firstpage.e.message: My stream is buffering for viewers.
I am dropping frames while streaming.
I'm seeing video related errors in my logs.
My server hardware is being over-utilized.
Chat is being shown as offline.
I want to reduce the live latency of my stream.
My stream's output is low quality.
My broadcasting software immediately disconnects after I start streaming.
I want to reset my stream key or admin password.
I have no idea what's going on. Can't I just talk to somebody?

wizard.troubleshooting.pages.streaming-p.message: Is buffering affecting everyone or only some viewers?
My stream is buffering for everyone.
My stream is fine for some, but buffering for others.

wizard.troubleshooting.pages.buffering-f.message: Viewers that are geographically far from my server.
Viewers on slower or mobile networks.

wizard.troubleshooting.pages.chat-offlin.message: Chat is offline only when I'm not streaming.

wizard.troubleshooting.pages.result_stre.message:
Recommended Guide
